title: Capstone Case Study 1 - Phase 3 Configuration & Integration

sections.content: The Business Blueprint has been approved. The SAP TM project now enters the Realize phase. Configuration activities begin while developers work on interfaces and Basis prepares the system landscape.

sections.background: The customer wants automatic planning every hour. The planning profile must consolidate deliveries heading to the same destination while respecting truck capacity and delivery commitments.
questions: Should planning be manual or automatic?

sections.title: Planning Strategy
sections.question: Which planning strategy should be configured?

options.text: Automatic optimization every hour with planner review for exceptions
Create Freight Orders immediately after every delivery

sections.explanation: Automatic planning improves efficiency while allowing planners to handle exceptions manually.

sections.subject: Planning Issue
sections.body: Several Freight Units were generated but were assigned to trucks exceeding legal vehicle capacity. Please investigate before tomorrow's testing.

sections.content: During integration testing, planners discover that deliveries from Pune never generate Freight Units. Hyderabad deliveries work correctly.

sections.title: Customer Escalation
sections.question: The customer asks whether configuration should continue before fixing the defect.

options.text: Continue configuration and ignore the issue.
Pause testing, resolve the defect and then continue.
Ask users to manually create Freight Orders.

sections.explanation: Critical integration defects should be resolved before further testing to avoid cascading issues.

notes: Pune integration defect assigned for correction.

sections.title: Real Project Advice
sections.content: Never move configuration into testing without documenting every configuration object. Proper documentation significantly reduces production support effort after Go-Live.

sections.title: Production Lesson
sections.content: Many SAP TM implementation issues are caused by incomplete master data or incorrect organizational assignments rather than software defects. Validate business configuration before assuming technical failure.

sections.title: Accenture Scenario
sections.content: During Realize phase, Freight Units are generated for one plant but not another. As an SAP TM consultant, explain your investigation approach, including master data validation, transportation relevance, organizational assignments, integration monitoring and configuration review.

sections.title: Phase 3 Complete
sections.content: Configuration activities are substantially complete. Integration defects have been identified and assigned. The project is ready to enter System Integration Testing (SIT).
